$comment: Decisions that must be made before Huginn is released to anyone (ADR-PROJ-002). `npm run release:check` FAILS while any entry is unresolved, and it is the first step of any release build. It is deliberately NOT part of check:all: a blocker must not redden every development build, or the next agent learns to ignore gates. Closing a blocker means setting resolved:true and writing the decision into `resolution` — deleting an entry to go green is falsifying a record (rule:code-quality).

blockers.title: Decide the publisher — company (lysis.ai) or the maintainer's own GitHub org
blockers.why: It fixes the bundle identifier. macOS keys the granted microphone and accessibility permissions to it; changing it after a release takes those permissions away from every existing user. The current value `ai.lysis.huginn` is a WORKING value, not a decision.
blockers.how: Set `identifier` + `vendor` in app.identity.json, run `npm run identity:sync`, then set resolved:true here.

blockers.title: Apple Developer Program (~99 USD/year)
blockers.why: Unavoidable for ANY macOS distribution, App Store or not: notarisation needs a Developer ID certificate, and without it Gatekeeper blocks the app on the user's machine. Autostart via SMAppService also requires a signed app, so that feature cannot even be verified until this exists.
blockers.how: Enrol, register the App ID for the final identifier, put the signing secrets into the release workflow (ADR-APP-023), then set resolved:true.

blockers.title: Decide the project licence
blockers.why: It decides the cargo-deny allow-list and therefore which dependencies may ever be used. Adding a copyleft dependency before this is decided can foreclose the answer.
blockers.how: Decide, put the text in LICENSE, align `src-tauri/deny.toml`'s licence allow-list, then set resolved:true.

blockers.title: Name and trademark check for 'Huginn'
blockers.why: The product brief demands it, and a prominent open-source project already carries the name. Publishing under a contested name is a problem that gets more expensive the later it is found.
blockers.how: Run the checks the brief lists (software names, trademarks in the target markets, domains, app-store names, GitHub orgs), record the result in docs/product/name-and-identity.md, then set resolved:true.

blockers.title: Huginn's own design system replaces the template's HUD look
blockers.why: The repo carried saga's neon/chamfer palette as an inherited placeholder (ADR-PROJ-003). Shipping it would be shipping another product's face.
blockers.how: Land Huginn's own design system (palette, radii, borders, typeface, primitives) in place of saga's neon/chamfer look, and replace the app icon under src-tauri/icons/** with Huginn's own mark, then set resolved:true here.
blockers.resolution: DONE. The design system landed 2026-07-13 (ADR-PROJ-003): a slate palette with a single steel accent in globals.css + palette.ts (light and dark), quiet radii, hairline borders, the system typeface; the neon glow, chamfer clip-paths, animated window frame and Orbitron font are gone, and every control is a primitive in src/components/ui/**. The app icon and tray mark are Huginn's own (commit 8c163bc): a sound-wave-in-a-wing mark generated by `npm run icons` from the two masters in assets/, replacing the template's mark across the ICO/ICNS and every PNG. Confirmed final on 2026-07-14.
